lede: A look at March 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ron Chernow and Bonnie J. Dunbar, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: De Havilland Comet
events.desc: A De Havilland Comet (Canadian Pacific Air Lines) crashes in Karachi, Pakistan, killing 11 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Ten Japanese warplanes raid Broome, Western Australia, killing more than 100 people.

events.title: Mumbai
events.desc: In Bombay, Mohandas Gandhi begins a hunger strike in protest at the autocratic rule in British India.

events.title: Petroleum
events.desc: Oil is discovered in Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic
events.desc: Russia signs the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk, agreeing to withdraw from World War I, and conceding German control of the Baltic States, Belarus and Ukraine. It also conceded Turkish control of Ardahan, Kars and Batumi.

events.title: Woman Suffrage Procession
events.desc: Thousands of women march in the Woman Suffrage Procession in Washington, D.C.

events.title: Shoshone National Forest
events.desc: Shoshone National Forest is established as the first national forest in the US and world.

events.title: Florida
events.desc: Florida is admitted as the 27th U.S. state.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The first amphibious landing of the United States Marine Corps begins the Battle of Nassau.

events.title: Mughal Empire
events.desc: Mughal Emperor Akbar defeats Sultan of Bengal Daud Khan Karrani's army at the Battle of Tukaroi.

births.name: Alexander Graham Bell
births.line: Scottish-American engineer and academic, invented the telephone

deaths.text: Gerhard Herzberg, German-Canadian chemist and astronomer, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Anselm, Duke of Friuli
Christian feast day: John and Charles Wesley (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Marinus and Asterius of Caesarea
